name: exponential_backoff_sequence
description: Reconnect loop with exact backoff: 2, 4, 8, 16, 30, 30... seconds
prompt: Write the reconnect loop with exponential backoff for the TradeOS WebSocket — it needs to retry with delays of 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, then cap at 30s, using asyncio.sleep(). Each attempt must be logged. From attempt 5 onward it should also send a Telegram alert. Reset the counter when reconnect succeeds.

expectations.text: Uses asyncio.sleep() (never time.sleep()) for all delays
Delay sequence is exactly 2, 4, 8, 16, 30, 30... (min(2**attempt, 30) formula or equivalent)
Logs each attempt with attempt number and wait time
Sends Telegram alert from attempt 5 onward (not attempt 1-4)
Resets reconnect_attempt counter to 0 on successful reconnect

name: stale_signal_discard
description: 5-minute stale signal detection with IST timezone
prompt: Write the stale signal detection check for after WebSocket reconnect — signals older than 5 minutes must be discarded with a WARNING log showing the age, and fresh signals must pass through normally. Use IST timezone.

expectations.text: Compares signal.generated_at against datetime.now() in IST timezone
Threshold is exactly 5 minutes (timedelta(minutes=5))
Stale signals are discarded with WARNING log showing signal age
Fresh signals pass through without being discarded
Uses pytz Asia/Kolkata for IST timezone (not UTC offset or hardcoded +5:30)

name: on_ticks_non_blocking
description: KiteTicker on_ticks callback must be non-blocking — push to queue immediately
prompt: Write the on_ticks callback for KiteTicker — it needs to pass tick data to the asyncio queue without doing any processing. KiteTicker runs in its own thread so you need the right bridge pattern.

expectations.text: Uses loop.call_soon_threadsafe() to bridge from KiteTicker thread to asyncio
Calls queue.put_nowait (not queue.put which is a coroutine)
Zero processing logic inside on_ticks — no validation, strategy, or order placement
on_ticks is a plain function (not async def)
Updates last_tick_timestamp in shared state from the callback

name: heartbeat_silent_disconnect_detection
description: Async heartbeat task that detects silent disconnects via tick silence
prompt: Write the heartbeat monitor that detects silent WebSocket disconnects — it should run as an async task checking every 30 seconds if a tick has been received. If no tick arrived in the last 30 seconds during market hours, trigger a reconnect.

expectations.text: Runs as an async task using asyncio.sleep(30) not time.sleep()
Checks last_tick_timestamp from shared ws_state dict
Triggers reconnect (sets ws_connected=False or calls reconnect()) when silence > 30s
Only checks during market hours (09:15–15:30 IST) — skips outside market hours
Logs WARNING when silent disconnect is detected with elapsed time

name: kill_switch_trigger_at_60s
description: Level 2 kill switch fires after 60 seconds of WebSocket disconnect during market hours
prompt: Write the disconnect handler that triggers Level 2 kill switch after 60 seconds of WebSocket being down — it records the disconnect timestamp when the connection drops, waits to see if it stays down, and if we're still disconnected after 60 seconds during market hours, calls kill_switch.trigger(level=2, reason='ws_timeout_60s').

expectations.text: Records disconnect_timestamp when WebSocket goes down (IST-aware datetime)
Checks elapsed time >= 60 seconds before triggering
Only triggers kill switch during market hours (09:15–15:30 IST)
Calls kill switch at level=2 with reason='ws_timeout_60s' (or equivalent)
Uses asyncio.sleep() (not time.sleep()) to wait and monitor

name: reconnect_resubscribes_all_instruments
description: on_connect callback re-subscribes watchlist and resets shared state
prompt: Write the on_connect callback for after a successful KiteTicker reconnect — it needs to re-subscribe to all instruments from the watchlist, set ws_connected=True in shared state, reset the reconnect_attempt counter, and log INFO with the instrument count.

expectations.text: Calls ticker.subscribe(instrument_tokens) and ticker.set_mode() to re-subscribe
Sets ws_connected=True in shared state via loop.call_soon_threadsafe()
Resets reconnect_attempt to 0 in shared state
Logs INFO with the count of instruments subscribed
Uses MODE_QUOTE (not MODE_FULL or MODE_LTP) for Phase 1

name: no_reconnect_after_market_close
description: Reconnect loop stops after 15:30 IST market close
prompt: Write the reconnect logic that stops after market close — during market hours 09:15-15:30 IST it should keep retrying, but once the market closes at 15:30 it should stop attempting reconnects and log that the market is closed.

expectations.text: Checks current IST time against market close (15:30) before each reconnect
Stops retrying after 15:30 IST — exits or returns from the reconnect loop
Logs INFO when stopping due to market close (not ERROR or WARNING)
Uses IST timezone (pytz Asia/Kolkata) for market hours comparison
Continues retrying during market hours (09:15–15:30) without stopping

name: thread_to_asyncio_bridge
prompt: KiteTicker runs in a thread but our signal processor is in the asyncio event loop. Write the bridge that safely passes tick data from the KiteTicker thread into the asyncio queue — show how to get the event loop reference and use it correctly.

expectations.text: Uses loop.call_soon_threadsafe() for thread-safe communication
Gets the running event loop with asyncio.get_event_loop() or asyncio.get_running_loop()
Does NOT call queue.put() directly from thread (would need await)
on_ticks callback is a plain function (not async def — KiteTicker calls it synchronously)
Returns immediately from on_ticks without blocking — no I/O or heavy computation
